rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in implementation scope when binding to implementation-level features or ports.
Preserve AADL structure by not introducing new components or altering connections during annex insertion.
generic_rules: Anchor AGREE variables to explicitly defined AADL ports or constants; do not assume implicit visibility.
Scope AGREE contracts to match the granularity of the target component (type vs. implementation).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Use `eq`, `assume`, or `guarantee` only for variables visible in the current scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly mapped to ports or constants.
Avoid using subcomponent ports directly in component-type annexes without intermediate bindings.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
